pattern: Every cell on the same diagonal shares the value row-minus-column, so grouping cells by that difference turns 'sort each diagonal' into independent sorts you can write straight back into the grid.
hints: All cells (r, c) with the same value of r - c lie on the same diagonal, running top-left to bottom-right.
Collect each diagonal's values into a list; diagonals with r >= c (the bottom-left triangle, including the main diagonal) get sorted non-increasing, the rest get sorted non-decreasing.
Walk each diagonal's coordinates again in the same top-left-to-bottom-right order you collected them, and overwrite the grid with the sorted values in that order.

edge_inputs.description: maximum size, already sorted correctly on every diagonal
duplicate values on a diagonal must still land in the required order
negative values mixed with positive ones on the same diagonal

stress.description: random square grids across the allowed size and value range
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = rng.randint(1, max(1, min(size, 10)))
    grid = [[rng.randint(-100000, 100000) for _ in range(n)] for _ in range(n)]
    return [grid]
